description_long: This is a weathered, cylindrical plastic bucket with a tightly fitted lid, showing significant signs of use and exposure to the elements. The surface is covered in peeling paint, dirt, and grime, with patches of the original gray and white plastic visible beneath. Three prominent horizontal reinforcement rings encircle the upper portion, providing structural strength and a visual break in the otherwise smooth body. Faint remnants of printed labels and stickers are visible, some partially torn or faded, indicating prior contents or usage instructions. The lid is slightly domed and appears to be made of the same material as the body, with a subtle raised pattern and a small central indentation. The bucket sits on a greenish, irregular base, likely for display or stabilization, but not part of the bucket itself.
description: A heavily used, gray plastic bucket with a fitted lid, peeling paint, and visible reinforcement rings. Faded labels and grime cover its weathered surface.
description_view_0: Front view shows the bucket's lid, three reinforcement rings, and a large patch of peeling paint with some label remnants.
description_view_1: This side reveals more extensive paint wear and a smoother, less marked surface, with the lid's edge and rings still visible.
description_view_2: The back displays a large, mostly intact label and more uniform gray coloration, with less peeling paint than other sides.
description_view_3: This view highlights the dirtiest, most weathered side, with dark stains, heavy grime, and a small yellow sticker.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: plastic reinforcement rings around upper body
attribute_labeling_or_markings: faded printed labels, stickers, and paint remnants
